prompt:
Passage 1
A regional equipment rental company recently changed how it tracks returned tools. Previously, employees checked tools back in by writing the tool name and return date on a paper sheet. If a tool was damaged or missing accessories, that information was sometimes written in the margin or reported verbally to a supervisor.
The company introduced a digital return checklist for each tool category. When a tool is returned, employees select the tool type, confirm that required accessories are present, and record any visible damage. The system then updates the tool’s availability status.
Managers report that the new process has reduced confusion about which tools are ready to rent again. It also helps the maintenance team identify damaged items before they are assigned to another customer.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: The company stopped renting tools to customers.
choices.B: A digital return checklist improved tracking of returned tools.
choices.C: Employees now repair all damaged tools immediately.
choices.D: Paper return sheets were used only for accessories.

explanation: The passage focuses on how a digital return checklist improved how returned tools are checked, tracked, and made available for rental again.

prompt:
Passage 2
MEMO
To: All Service Department Employees
Subject: Updated Customer Callback Procedure
Beginning June 10, service employees must record customer callback requests in the shared service log. Each entry should include the customer’s name, phone number, reason for the call, and the employee assigned to respond.
The change was made because callback requests were previously recorded in several different places, including notebooks, email messages, and individual phone notes. This made it difficult for supervisors to confirm whether every customer had received a response.
Employees should check the shared log at the start and end of each shift.

What is the purpose of the memo?

choices.A: To announce a new customer callback procedure
choices.B: To cancel customer phone service
choices.C: To introduce a new employee uniform

explanation: The memo announces the updated customer callback procedure and explains how employees should record requests.

prompt:
Passage 3
Many warehouses use cycle counting to monitor inventory accuracy. Instead of counting every item in the warehouse at one time, employees count selected groups of items on a regular schedule. Over time, this allows the company to check inventory records without stopping normal operations for a full physical count.
Cycle counting can help identify problems such as misplaced items, incorrect quantities, or recording errors. If a certain product category repeatedly shows differences between the system record and the actual shelf count, managers may review receiving, picking, or stocking procedures.
The method works best when counts are performed carefully and results are investigated. Simply recording differences without looking for causes may not improve inventory accuracy.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Warehouses should never count inventory.
choices.B: Cycle counting helps warehouses monitor inventory accuracy without stopping operations.
choices.C: Full physical counts are required every day.

explanation: The passage explains how cycle counting lets warehouses check inventory accuracy regularly without stopping normal operations.

prompt:
Passage 4
Before modern traffic signals became common, busy intersections were often controlled by police officers or simple signs. As automobile traffic increased, this approach became harder to manage. Drivers sometimes had difficulty knowing when to proceed, especially when traffic came from several directions.
Traffic signals helped create a more orderly system for controlling intersections. By assigning right-of-way through red, yellow, and green lights, signals reduced confusion and helped traffic move more predictably. Later improvements included pedestrian signals, turn arrows, and timing systems that could be adjusted for traffic conditions.
Today, traffic signals remain an important part of road safety. Engineers continue to review signal timing and intersection design to improve safety and reduce delays.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Traffic signals helped make intersections safer and more orderly.
choices.B: Police officers no longer work near roads.
choices.C: Automobiles are no longer used in cities.
choices.D: Pedestrian signals caused traffic systems to fail.

explanation: The passage explains how traffic signals made intersections more orderly and continue to support road safety.

prompt:
Passage 5
A regional packaging company recently began using moisture-resistant labels on shipments that may be exposed to damp conditions. Previously, standard paper labels were used on all shipments. During rainy weather or humid storage conditions, some labels became difficult to read, which occasionally delayed sorting and delivery.
The new labels are designed to remain readable even when exposed to moisture. Shipping employees tested the labels on several product lines before the company expanded their use. Managers found that the labels were especially helpful for outdoor deliveries and warehouse areas where temperature changes caused condensation.
Although the new labels cost more than standard paper labels, company leaders believe the added expense is justified if it reduces delivery problems and customer complaints.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: The company stopped using labels on shipments.
choices.B: Moisture-resistant labels helped reduce problems caused by unreadable shipping labels.
choices.C: Customers now sort all packages themselves.
choices.D: Standard paper labels work best in every condition.

explanation: The passage focuses on how moisture-resistant labels reduce problems caused by labels becoming unreadable in damp conditions.

prompt:
Passage 6
Many organizations use document version control to avoid confusion when several people work on the same file. Version control helps users identify the most current document and track changes made over time.
Without version control, employees may accidentally use outdated instructions, duplicate someone else’s work, or send customers incorrect information. A clear system may include file names with dates, revision numbers, approval notes, or a shared location where current documents are stored.
Version control works best when everyone follows the same rules. If some employees save separate copies in personal folders while others use the shared system, confusion can continue.

What is the main idea of the passage?

choices.A: Document version control helps prevent confusion when files are updated or shared.
choices.B: Employees should keep all documents in personal folders.
choices.C: Customers should write company documents.
choices.D: Revision numbers make files impossible to use.

explanation: The passage explains that version control helps employees identify current documents and avoid confusion when files change.

prompt:
Passage 7
Many communities use public noise ordinances to manage sound levels in residential and commercial areas. These rules may limit loud construction work, amplified music, vehicle noise, or other activities during certain hours.
The purpose of a noise ordinance is to balance different needs. Businesses and residents may need to complete work, hold events, or receive deliveries, but nearby people also need reasonable quiet, especially during nighttime hours. Clear rules help reduce disputes by setting expectations for when loud activities are allowed.
Enforcement often depends on complaints, sound measurements, and local procedures. Communities may review ordinances when growth, new businesses, or repeated complaints show that existing rules need adjustment.

What is the passage mainly about?

choices.A: How communities use noise ordinances to balance activity and quiet
choices.B: Why all construction work should be banned
choices.C: How vehicles are manufactured
choices.D: Why businesses should ignore local rules

explanation: The passage explains how noise ordinances balance the needs of businesses, residents, and public quiet.

prompt:
Passage 8
A technical training center recently studied why some students did not complete its evening certification course. The course was designed for adults who were preparing for entry-level technical jobs while continuing to work during the day.
Staff reviewed attendance records and surveyed students who withdrew. The most common reasons were schedule conflicts, transportation issues, and difficulty keeping up with weekly assignments. Several students said they understood the material during class but fell behind when they missed one or two sessions.
In response, the center added a weekly review period and created a system for students to request missed handouts and practice problems. Staff hope these changes will help students recover more quickly after absences.

What was the purpose of the study?

choices.A: To find out why some students did not complete the evening certification course
choices.B: To replace all evening courses with daytime classes
choices.C: To remove technical training from the center

explanation: The study was conducted to learn why some students did not complete the evening certification course.

Which statement is best supported by the passage?

choices.A: Support after absences may help adult students stay on track.
choices.B: Students who miss class never need review materials.
choices.C: Evening courses are designed only for people who do not work.
choices.D: Transportation issues cannot affect course completion.

explanation: The added review period and missed-material system support the idea that help after absences may keep adult students on track.
